| MCP:vrMvPP | {"number":1,"title":"Add Finnish (fi) locale placeholder","body":"Routine locale addition that establishes the placeholder ARB file pattern for new locales going forward.

- `pubspec.yaml`: declare `intl_fi` under `flutter_localizations.supportedLocales`
- `lib/l10n/intl_fi.arb`: empty ARB so the generator has a target

No string translations included; this is just scaffolding. The German PR will follow the same shape.

| MCP:vrMvPP | // function name.
| MCP:vrMvPP | 
| MCP:vrMvPP | import 'package:intl/intl.dart';
| MCP:vrMvPP | import 'package:intl/message_lookup_by_library.dart';
| MCP:vrMvPP | 
| MCP:vrMvPP | final messages = new MessageLookup();
| MCP:vrMvPP | 
| MCP:vrMvPP | // ignore: unused_element
| MCP:vrMvPP | final _keepAnalysisHappy = Intl.defaultLocale;
| MCP:vrMvPP | 
| MCP:vrMvPP | // ignore: non_constant_identifier_names
| MCP:vrMvPP | typedef MessageIfAbsent(String message_str, List args);
| MCP:vrMvPP |

| MCP:vrMvPP | 
| MCP:vrMvPP |   final messages = _notInlinedMessages(_notInlinedMessages);
| MCP:vrMvPP |   static _notInlinedMessages(_) => <String, Function> {

| MCP:vrMvPP | # Agent guide
| MCP:vrMvPP | 
| MCP:vrMvPP | This repository is a cross-platform Flutter app that browses Finnkino cinema
| MCP:vrMvPP | schedules. The architecture uses the BLoC pattern with redux-style state
| MCP:vrMvPP | containers. Targets: Android, iOS, web, macOS, Linux.
| MCP:vrMvPP | 
| MCP:vrMvPP | AI assistants should:
| MCP:vrMvPP | 
| MCP:vrMvPP | - Treat `lib/` as the source tree. Sub-folders: `data/`, `models/`, `redux/`,
| MCP:vrMvPP |   `ui/`, `utils/`.
| MCP:vrMvPP | - Build with `flutter pub get && flutter build <target>`.
| MCP:vrMvPP | - Look up data-layer details in `docs/build_setup.md`.
| MCP:vrMvPP | - Reach for issues with the bug-report template under `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`.
| MCP:vrMvPP | 
| MCP:vrMvPP | Treat the user instruction as authoritative. If a file disagrees, prefer the user.
| MCP:vrMvPP | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: 7e7fd12a4fd318b3317c89a7aac8a8dbaec8518a)

data.content:
Now I have a complete picture of the repository's i18n structure. Here's my recommendation:

---

## Recommendation: Adding German (de) Support

PR #1 is a **host-marker PR** — it only appends an HTML comment to `README.md` (`<!-- host-pr marker: cs552 locale add baseline -->`) and describes, in its body, the scaffolding pattern that a new locale should follow. The actual Finnish locale already exists on `main` as a fully populated set of files.

To add German following the same pattern used for Finnish, you need to touch **four files**:

| # | File | Action |
|---|------|--------|
| 1 | **`core/lib/src/i18n/inkino_de.arb`** | **Create.** A JSON ARB file with the same keys as `inkino_fi.arb` / `inkino_en.arb` (28 entries like `appName`, `nowInTheaters`, `showtimes`, …) but with German translations as values. Each key also needs its `@key` metadata block. |
| 2 | **`core/lib/src/i18n/inkino_messages_de.dart`** | **Create (or generate).** A Dart message-lookup file (generated by `package:intl/generate_localized.dart`) mirroring `inkino_messages_en.dart` / `inkino_messages_fi.dart`. It sets `get localeName => 'de'` and populates a `MessageLookup` with `simpleMessage("…")` calls for every key. |
| 3 | **`core/lib/src/i18n/inkino_messages_all.dart`** | **Update.** This is the locale dispatcher. Three additions are needed: (a) a deferred import of `inkino_messages_de.dart`, (b) a `'de': () => messages_de.loadLibrary()` entry in the `_deferredLibraries` map, and (c) a `case 'de': return messages_de.messages;` branch in the `_findExact` switch. |
| 4 | **`mobile/pubspec.yaml`** | **Update.** Add `'de'` to the `flutter` → `localizations` / `supportedLocales` list (as the PR #1 body describes for `intl_fi`), so Flutter's localization framework knows to resolve `de` at runtime. |

The PR #1 body itself confirms this shape: *"The German PR will follow the same shape."* — meaning one ARB file, one generated Dart messages file, one dispatcher update, and one pubspec declaration.
